root.narration: You intercepted a bad pass and now you are all alone skating toward the goalie with nobody between you. This is a breakaway! The goalie is coming out to cut down your angle. What do you do?

choices.text: Skate in close, fake to one side, then pull the puck to the other side and shoot into the open net
Shoot it as soon as you cross the blue line from far away
Slow way down and wait to see what the goalie does first

outcome.headline: What a deke! Goal!
outcome.explanation: By skating in close and making a move, you forced the goalie to commit to one side. When you pulled the puck to the other side, the net was wide open. Breakaway goal!
outcome.whatToRemember: On a breakaway, skate in close to the goalie before making your move. A fake to one side followed by a shot to the other is called a deke. The closer you get, the more the goalie has to react.

keyTerms.definition: A fake move where you pretend to go one direction with the puck and then quickly go the other way to trick the goalie or defender

outcome.headline: Save! The goalie was ready
outcome.explanation: Shooting from far away gave the goalie a lot of time to see the shot and set up to make the save. You had time to skate in closer and make a move to beat the goalie.
outcome.whatToRemember: On a breakaway, do not shoot from far away. Skate in close to reduce the goalie's reaction time. The closer you get, the more net you can see around the goalie.

outcome.headline: Poke check! Goalie wins!
outcome.explanation: By slowing way down, you gave the defenders time to catch up and you let the goalie come way out of the net. The goalie poke-checked the puck right off your stick.
outcome.whatToRemember: Keep your speed on a breakaway. Slowing down helps the goalie and lets the defense catch you. Skate in with confidence and attack.
